criteria.label: State H(P, Q) and compute it for small discrete P and Q.
criteria.example: P = [0.5, 0.5], Q = [0.9, 0.1]: compute −Σ p log q in nats and compare to H(P).

criteria.label: Derive −log q(y) from one-hot cross-entropy for classification.
criteria.example: Write P as one-hot on class 2 and show only the k=2 term survives in −Σ p_k log q_k.

criteria.label: Implement or call categorical cross-entropy on logits and verify against a framework loss.
criteria.example: Match F.cross_entropy(logits, y) to manual log_softmax plus gather of −log q(y) on a 3-class toy batch.

criteria.label: Explain H(P, Q) = H(P) + D_KL(P || Q) and when minimizing CE equals minimizing KL.
criteria.example: Argue that fixed training labels make H(P) constant, so CE and KL differ by a constant offset with the same minimizer.
